text:
According to Nikolai A. Voznesenskiy's book "The Military Economy of the USSR during the Patriotic War", released in 1947, was written by one of the most important officials in the Soviet government during wartime, and a Doctor of Economics. Voznesenskiy had been responsible for the majority of the work in preparing the Soviet military economy for war, and in achieving the war's early industrial and strategic goals. He played a central role in the planning and achievement of the Soviet goals of wartime expansion, including the conversion of the country's main industrial area to the far eastern regions and the establishing a new war industry in the Urals, Siberia and Central Asia; all of which were necessary in order to achieve the ultimate Soviet goal of producing more war materials than Germany and the Axis.

The text provides extensive information on the drastic measures undertaken in Soviet Union to restructure the economy for "total war", describing the removal of more than 1300 large enterprises and the rapid development of tank, aircraft and armament production. Voznesenskiy also played a major role in the Committee for the Restoration of the National Economy, the institution that managed the enormous and highly labor-intensive efforts to rebuild and modernize the infrastructure, agriculture and dwellings of the liberated areas. These primary sources provide a wealth of detailed information from first hand sources and thus enables the student to understand in great detail how the command economy of the Soviet Union was able to effectively deal with the enormous territorial losses it had suffered and thereby reached technical and numerical equality on the battlefields.
